summary: AuthPage (/auth) and LoginPage (/login) UI/UX redesign testing COMPLETE - ALL TESTS PASSED. Both pages correctly implement split-screen layout (50/50 desktop, stacked mobile), dark header with logo on #0B0B0D background, white form body, gold CTA buttons, 3D tilt effect. All authentication functionality preserved and working.

tests.test: Split-screen layout with image left, card right
Left panel shows hero image with Ken Burns animation
Google login button visible and clickable (data-testid='auth-google-btn')
'Continue with Email' button enabled when email entered (data-testid='auth-send-otp-btn')
'Admin / RM / Venue Login' link navigates to /login (data-testid='auth-team-login-link')
Left image panel hidden on mobile
All form elements visible and accessible
Google button, email input, CTA all visible
OTP container visible after sending code (data-testid='auth-otp-container')
'Check your inbox and spam folder' hint visible (data-testid='auth-check-inbox-hint')
Back button returns to email step
Toast shows 'Verification code sent — check your inbox'
Split-screen layout with abstract dark/gold left, card right
Left panel shows abstract gradient with gold accents
Dark header shows 'Team Portal' text and logo
Left abstract panel hidden on mobile
All form elements visible and accessible
'Welcome back!' toast shown on successful login

critical_code_review_comments: Minor: RMDashboard has hydration warning about <tr> inside <span> - unrelated to auth page redesign, pre-existing issue

mocked_apis.note: All APIs are live - no mocked endpoints

context_for_next_testing_agent: Auth pages redesign verified. Key data-testids: auth-card, auth-brand-logo, auth-google-btn, auth-email-input, auth-send-otp-btn, auth-stay-signed-in, auth-team-login-link, auth-back-btn, auth-otp-container, auth-otp-input-{0-5}, auth-verify-otp-btn, auth-check-inbox-hint, auth-resend-otp-btn, auth-change-email-btn. Login page: login-card, full-brand-logo, login-email, login-password, login-toggle-password, login-submit, login-customer-link, login-back-btn. Both pages use framer-motion for entrance animations and CSS transforms for 3D tilt on card hover.
